body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

turn-end-terminal-text asserts the smallest tool-less round-trip and pins the adapter-specific end-of-turn signal: the assistant produces a final plain-text message and the parser resolves the session back to ready. The user-observable signal is the canonical ready → working → ready arc, reached regardless of which adapter token closes the turn.

For opencode the terminal-text end signal is the step-finish part with reason="stop". The parser maps it to a turn_done event (parser.go parseStepFinish: `case "stop": ev.EventType = "turn_done"`), which drives the session working→ready. Session state lives in opencode's SQLite store (~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db); the daemon's ProcessOwnedStore source polls the session table and observes the UUID-keyed ses_… session for the cwd.

The committed sibling recordings (basic-turn, long-idle-live-session) confirm a single `opencode run` produces exactly this arc on one ses_… session: new_state=ready → working → ready. Because the prompt is tool-less and carries no trailing question, the working span carries no auto-executed tool call and the session never enters waiting.

The interactive driver (drive-opencode-interactive.sh) launches a blocking `opencode run` per send and passes NO --model flag, so opencode uses its configured default (a local LM Studio model). turn-end-terminal-text is a single plain-text turn (one send + wait_turn no-op + trailing sleep) — well within this grammar, so driver_capability: ready.

MODEL note: opencode's default model is the local LM Studio model `lmstudio/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored`, set in ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json. It is small and slow (~30–45s per turn), so the recipe budgets generous timeouts (180s) and a trailing sleep that outlasts the turn_done settle.
caveats: OpenCode does not expose CLI-assignable session IDs; the driver discovers the session id from SQLite (most recent row keyed on directory) after the first `opencode run` returns.
The local gemma model is slow (~30–45s per turn); the recipe uses a 180s timeout and a generous trailing sleep so a slow turn does not trip a false timeout or miss the working→ready settle.

sources.note: parseStepFinish: reason="stop" → EventType "turn_done" closes the turn (working→ready). This is the adapter-specific terminal-text end signal this cell pins.
Source variant ProcessOwnedStore (SQLite at ~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db) with ExactName{Name: "opencode"} process matcher; daemon polls the session table for the ses_… session.
Sibling recording: a single `opencode run` emits state_transition new_state ready→working→ready on one ses_… session — the same arc this cell records, with the spec framing the end-signal mechanism.
